DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS PREMATURE AND THEY HAVE NOT MADE A SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF'S ACTIONS REGARDING THE PRIVILEGE LOG RISES TO THE LEVEL OF A "MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS". THE PARTIES SHOULD CONTINUE TO MEET AND CONFER TO ESTABLISH A DEADLINE FOR DETERMINING WHAT DOCUMENTS WILL BE PRODUCED OR BE DEFINITELY EXCLUDED (PLAINTIFF HAS ALREADY STATED THAT IT WOULD NOT SEEK ADMISSION OF PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS) AND WHEN THEY WILL BE PRODUCED. OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY EUREKA GARDEN HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT DANMEIER, DONALD WILLIAM DANMEIER ARCHITECTS, A PROFESSIONAL CORPORAION KLUNIS, MATTHEW N.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DONALD DANMEIER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER OR NOT PLAINTIFFS CONTRACTED WITH DEFENDANT AS AN INDIVIDUAL OR PROFESSIONAL CORP. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PROVIDE ANY EVIDENCE THAT AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY EXISTS WARRANTING DECLARATORY RELIEF. ALTER EGO LIABILITY IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION AND EVEN IF IT WERE, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THIS CLAIM. THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER OF DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE UNDER SUBMISSION WITH THE UNDERSTANDING THAT SHOULD THE RULINGS CAUSE THE COURT TO CHANGE ITS RULING ON THE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION MOTION, THE MATTER WILL BE RE-SET FOR HEARING. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED ON DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS AS FOLLOWS: DONALD DANMEIER'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY EUREKA GARDEN HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE OVERRULED. COUNSEL NOTIFIED BY PHONE OF THE RULING ON AUGUST 4, 2011. MR. FANT IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-03-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

DECLARATION OF J. MICHAEL HIGGINBOTHAM IN OPPOSITION TO KLUNIS' MTN FOR SUMM ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF EUREKA GARDEN HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION
DECLARATION OF SUZANNE EISENHUT IN OPPOSITION TO KLUNIS' MTN FOR SUMM ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF EUREKA GARDEN HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION
RESPONSE TO DEFT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT IN OPPOSITION TO KLUNIS' MTN FOR SUMM ADJUDICATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF EUREKA GARDEN HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION

LAW AND MOTION, DEFENDANT MATTHEW N. KLUNIS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - DEFENDANT KLUNIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS GRANTED BECAUSE HE IS NOT A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT. FURTHER THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT IS A "PARTNER" IN THE PARTNERSHIP OR THAT THERE WAS A PARTNERSHIP FORMED UNDER THE CORPORATIONS CODE. DEFENDANT KLUNIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE IS GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT AT ALL MATERIAL TIMES DEFENDANT WAS AN EMPLOYEE OF DANMEIER ARCHITECTS AND NOT A LICENSED ARCHITECT. FURTHER PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRODUCE EVIDENCE OF PHYSICAL DAMAGE THEREBY ENTITLING DEFENDANT TO PROTECTION UNDER THE AGENT IMMUNITY DOCTRINE. DEFENDANT KLUNIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION IS GRANTED. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT THIS DEFENDANT MADE THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS, THAT DEFENDANT WAS ACTING, AT ALL RELEVANT TIMES, SOLELY AS AN EMPLOYEE OF DANEIER ARCHITECTS, AND BECAUSE HE DID NOT CAUSE PHYSICAL DAMAGE TO PLAINTIFF, HE IS ENTITLED TO PROTECTION UNDER THE IMMUNITY DOCTRINE. DEFENDANT KLUNIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR "ALTER-EGO" LIABILITY IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH BOTH THE REQUISITE UNITY OF INTEREST BETWEEN DEFENDANT AND THE ALLEGED CORPORATION AND THE REQUISITE BAD FAITH NECESSARY TO ESTABLISH LIABILITY AGAINST HIM. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302)

Off Calendar Aug-19-2011 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DONALD DANMEIER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: J. Michael Higginbotham of Long & Levit LLP (397.2222) for plaintiff and responding party; Philip A. Fant (747.8787) for defendant and moving party.
Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: defendant's motion for summary adjudication of the 1st and 2nd cause of action is denied. Plaintiff has raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether or not plaintiffs contracted with defendant as an individual or professional corp. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication of the 5th and 6th causes of action is granted. Plaintiff does not provide any evidence that an actual controversy exists warranting declaratory relief. Alter ego liability is not a cause of action and even if it were, plaintiff has presented no admissible evidence to support this claim. The court took the matter of defendant's objections to evidence under submission with the understanding that should the rulings cause the court to change its ruling on the summary adjudication motion, the matter will be re-set for hearing. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
---
After hearing, the court ruled on defendant's objections as follows: DONALD DANMEIER'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY EUREKA GARDEN HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION are overruled. Counsel notified by phone of the ruling on August 4, 2011. Mr. Fant is directed to submit a proposed form of order.

MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Evidence Sanction For Misuse Of Discovery
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT DANMEIER ARCHITECTS, A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR EVIDENCE SANCTION FOR MISUSE OF DISCOVERY.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR EVIDENTIARY SANCTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' MOTION IS PREMATURE AND THEY HAVE NOT MADE A SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF'S ACTIONS REGARDING THE PRIVILEGE LOG RISES TO THE LEVEL OF A "MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS". THE PARTIES SHOULD CONTINUE TO MEET AND CONFER TO ESTABLISH A DEADLINE FOR DETERMINING WHAT DOCUMENTS WILL BE PRODUCED OR BE DEFINITELY EXCLUDED (PLAINTIFF HAS ALREADY STATED THAT IT WOULD NOT SEEK ADMISSION OF PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS) AND WHEN THEY WILL BE PRODUCED. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT MATTHEW N. KLUNIS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: J. Michael Higginbotham, Esq. of Long & Levit LLP 397-2222 for pltf & opposing party; Philip A. Fant, Esq. of Alper & McCulloch 785-8814 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted -
Defendant Klunis' Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 1st and 2nd causes of action for breach of contract is granted because he is not a party to the contract. Further there is no evidence that defendant is a "partner" in the partnership or that there was a partnership formed under the Corporations Code.
Defendant Klunis' Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 3rd cause of action for professional negligence is granted. The evidence shows that at all material times defendant was an employee of Danmeier Architects and not a licensed architect. Further plaintiff fails to produce evidence of physical damage thereby entitling defendant to protection under the agent immunity doctrine.
Defendant Klunis' Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 4th cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is granted. There is no evidence that this defendant made the alleged misrepresentations, that defendant was acting, at all relevant times, solely as an employee of Daneier Architects, and because he did not cause physical damage to plaintiff, he is entitled to protection under the immunity doctrine.
Defendant Klunis' Motion for Summary Adjudication on the 6th cause of action for "alter-ego" liability is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish both the requisite unity of interest between defendant and the alleged corporation and the requisite bad faith necessary to establish liability against him.
PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
